findings.description: Header text is duplicated and the page footer is inserted before the main document title, disrupting reading order.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, header text is only at the top and the footer is at the bottom. In the HTML, the footer and a duplicated block of header text appear between the top logo/header and the 'MEMORANDUM' title.

findings.description: Footer content is incorrectly inserted into the main text flow.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the text flows directly from the outline to the first section. In the HTML, the footer text and colored bar are inserted between the outline and the 'I. History/Background' heading.

findings.description: Footer content breaks a continuous sentence in the main text flow.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, a bullet point reads '...Coal ash should be disposed of...'. In the HTML, this sentence is interrupted in the middle by the footer text and colored bar.

findings.description: Footer content is incorrectly inserted into the middle of a section.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the text flows from a paragraph to an introductory sentence for a list within Section III. In the HTML, the footer text and colored bar interrupt this flow.

findings.description: Footer content is incorrectly inserted into the main text flow between sections.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, Section IV immediately follows the end of the Section III list. In the HTML, the footer text and colored bar from the previous page are inserted before the 'IV. Attachments' heading.
